contents.value: **program** — Pascal keyword.
**unit** — Pascal keyword.
**interface** — Pascal keyword.
**implementation** — Pascal keyword.
**uses** — Pascal keyword.
**WriteLn** — Built-in function/command in Pascal.
**ReadLn** — Built-in function/command in Pascal.
**Write** — Built-in function/command in Pascal.
**Read** — Built-in function/command in Pascal.
**Inc** — Built-in function/command in Pascal.
A **procedure** is a subroutine that does not return a value.
A **function** is a subroutine that returns a value.

**var** declares variables.
```pascal
var x: Integer; s: String;
```
**const** declares compile-time constants.
**type** defines custom types, records, or enumerations.

A **record** groups related fields.
```pascal
type TPoint = record x, y: Integer; end;
```

An **array** stores fixed or dynamic sequences.
```pascal
var a: array[1..10] of Integer;
```
**begin..end** delimits a compound statement (block).
**if..then..else** provides conditional branching.
**for..do** iterates with a counter variable.
**while..do** repeats while a condition is true.
**repeat..until** loops at least once until condition is true.

```pascal
Integer
```
The standard signed integer type in Pascal, typically 16-bit in Turbo Pascal or 32-bit in Free Pascal and Delphi. Supports arithmetic operations, comparisons, and ordinal functions.

```pascal
String[maxLength]
```
The character string type in Pascal, storing a sequence of characters with a length byte prefix. Default maximum length is 255 characters. Supports concatenation, comparison, and index access.

```pascal
Real
```
The floating-point numeric type in Pascal for storing decimal numbers. Typically 48-bit in Turbo Pascal or mapped to Double (64-bit) in Free Pascal and Delphi for IEEE 754 compliance.

```pascal
Boolean
```
The logical type in Pascal with exactly two values: True and False. Used in conditional expressions, control flow statements, and as function return types for predicate operations.

```pascal
Array[indexType] of elementType
```
A fixed-size collection of elements of the same type, indexed by an ordinal type range. Arrays in Pascal are declared with explicit bounds and support multidimensional and packed variants.

```pascal
Record field1: Type1; field2: Type2; end
```
A composite data type that groups related fields of different types under a single name. Records support variant parts, can be nested, and are used for structured data modeling in Pascal.

```pascal
TStringList = class(TStrings)
```
A powerful string collection class in the Classes unit providing sorted and unsorted string lists with name-value pair support, file I/O, and delimiter-separated text parsing capabilities.

```pascal
Ord(x: ordinal) -> Integer
```
Returns the ordinal value (integer position) of an ordinal-type expression, including characters, booleans, and enumeration values. For Char arguments, returns the ASCII code value.

```pascal
Chr(x: Byte) -> Char
```
Returns the character corresponding to the specified ASCII code value. The inverse of the Ord function, converting an integer byte value back to its character representation.

```pascal
Dec(var x: ordinal [, n: Integer])
```
Decrements an ordinal variable by 1 or by the optional amount n. More efficient than x := x - 1 as it generates optimized machine code for the decrement operation.

```pascal
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```pascal
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```pascal
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```pascal
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```pascal
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.

```pascal
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
